message:
feat(codec): add GetCodecVersionResponse.min_supported for symmetric handshake (ft-kuxho.B.3)

Final piece of the ft-kuxho.B arc. Adds an end-of-struct
`min_supported: usize` field to `GetCodecVersionResponse` so peers
exchange both their CODEC_VERSION and their CODEC_VERSION_MIN_SUPPORTED
at handshake time, enabling the rolling-upgrade window's symmetric
negotiation.

Wire-format choices (per docs/proposals/ft-kuxho-B-codec-version-min-supported-window.md):
  - Field appended at the END of the struct so older payloads still
    deserialize cleanly via #[serde(default)] — the strictly-additive
    case the proposal advocates and the ft-kuxho.B.2 conformance harness
    proves safe.
  - NO `skip_serializing_if`. Per MEMORY varbincode-skip-serializing-if-bug
    and the comment block at codec/src/lib.rs:1226, eliding fields in a
    positional binary format misaligns every downstream offset. Always
    serialize.
  - Default value is `0` as a sentinel meaning "no min advertised".
    The handshake call-site detects 0 and substitutes `codec_vers`,
    treating a legacy peer as supporting only its own version (the
    same conservative convention used in ft-kuxho.B.1's bootstrap).

Construction sites updated (~20 across the workspace):
  - frankenterm/codec/src/lib.rs (2 test sites)
  - crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/sessionhandler.rs (production
    server response)
  - crates/frankenterm-core/tests/vendored_async_contract_behavioral.rs
    (~17 test scenarios)
  - crates/frankenterm-core/benches/{compression_bypass,mux_client_ops,
    mux_pool_scaling}.rs (3 mock-server bench sites)

Client handshake (frankenterm/client/src/client.rs) now threads
`info.min_supported` into `codec::check_compat` with the legacy-0
fallback to `info.codec_vers`, completing the symmetric window
negotiation that ft-kuxho.B.1 stubbed and ft-kuxho.B.2 proved sound.

Tests:
  - pdu_roundtrip_get_codec_version_response asserts min_supported
    survives the wire byte-for-byte (3/3 get_codec_version-prefix tests
    pass).
  - cargo check across codec, wezterm-client, frankenterm-mux-server-impl
    clean.

Closes ft-kuxho.3. The ft-kuxho.B implementation arc (ft-kuxho.{1,2,3})
is now complete; the parent epic ft-kuxho can close once track A
follow-ups (release-note row at next bump, deprecation log line for
legacy peers) land.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-6mxfs","title":"[mor:security-engineering] ws_dir = root.join('.ft','patterns') is not canonicalized before discovery","description":"# Observation

In `load_packs_from_config`
(crates/frankenterm-core/src/patterns.rs:949):

    let ws_dir = root_path.join(".ft").join("patterns");
    let discovered = discover_packs_from_dir(&ws_dir)?;

ft-fbee5 (just closed) fixed the case where a symlink INSIDE
`.ft/patterns/` escapes the sandbox. But the parent path is itself
just a join, no canonicalize. A hostile cloned repo can ship:

    .ft/patterns -> /etc

(a symlink at the directory level.) `ws_dir` canonicalizes to
`/etc`, `discover_packs_from_dir(/etc)` iterates there, and the
per-file sandbox check passes because it canonicalizes with root =
`/etc`. The symlink escape is now one level up.

Similar attack surface for `config.resolved_user_packs_dir()` if
that ever returns a joined path the caller doesn't canonicalize.

# Question

Should `load_packs_from_config` canonicalize `ws_dir` and verify the
result resolves *inside* `root_path.canonicalize()` before calling
`discover_packs_from_dir`? That closes the parent-level symlink
vector without needing to change `discover_packs_from_dir`'s
contract.

Stretch: is there a case for centralizing this as a
`sandbox_resolve_dir(dir, root)` helper and using it everywhere the
pack/workflow/policy loaders join a workspace-relative path? The
pattern is probably not unique to pack loading.

# Trust boundary

The repo is the attacker; the user's home directory is the victim.
Everything a hostile repo can make `read_to_string` see is a
potential data-exfil primitive. Treat repo-relative path
construction as crossing a trust boundary, and canonicalize at the
boundary crossing, not at the leaf.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:35:09.320199Z","created_by":"mor-review","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:49.174562Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:51:59.305207Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 0239fbd6 — added sandbox_resolve_dir helper, routed load_packs_from_config's ws_dir through it with 4 regression tests (missing, inside-root, toplevel-symlink-escape, end-to-end).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["mor","sandboxing","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-6piwt","title":"[P0 BLOCKER] frankenterm-core lib has 61 compile errors under --no-default-features — blocks all --no-default-features test/bench runs","description":"## Symptom
`cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features` fails with 61 errors. Panes 3 and 4 in the swarm both hit this while trying to verify their respective commits.

## Representative error
```
error[E0599]: no method named `record_event_with_cx` found for this struct
  --> crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:6157
  help: there is a method `record_event` with a similar name

error[E0599]: no method named `get_text_with_cx` found for struct `std::sync::Arc<(dyn WeztermInterface + 'static)>`
  --> crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs:3272:43
  note: `wezterm::PaneTextSource` defines an item `get_text_with_cx`, perhaps you need to implement it
```

## Root cause hypothesis
Asupersync migration landed `_with_cx` **callers** in some files without landing `_with_cx` **implementations** on the corresponding traits/structs, specifically under the default feature set (where the legacy impls are gated off but the new impls aren't gated on).

## Scope
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs (record_event_with_cx)
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/wezterm.rs (get_text_with_cx + PaneTextSource trait alignment)
- 61 errors total; likely same pattern elsewhere

## Fix direction
1. Run `cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features 2>&1 | grep 'error\[\|error:' > /tmp/errors.txt` to enumerate all 61.
2. Group by method name; for each missing `*_with_cx` method:
   - If the non-cx variant exists, add a default impl or route `with_cx` through it (respects cancel semantics).
   - If neither exists, revert the caller to the non-cx variant with a TODO referencing ft-xbnl0.2 epic.
3. Verify with `cargo check` clean, then `cargo test --lib --no-default-features`.

## Priority
P0 — blocks swarm from running any tests under --no-default-features, which is the recommended lean test surface.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T14:29:56.725238Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:50:22.680762Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:50:22.680537Z","close_reason":"Added no-feature cx compatibility shims and ungated fallback *_with_cx adapters so frankenterm-core --lib --no-default-features compiles again","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-761tz","title":"[HIGH] disk_pressure NaN sample silently reports Green tier instead of failing closed","description":"# Defect

## Concrete failure path

1. Caller passes `DiskSample { usage_fraction: NaN, .. }` to `DiskPressureMonitor::update_with_sample`. Easy trigger: `available/total` when `total == 0` (e.g., disk fails to mount, statvfs momentarily returns zero, unit-test fixture with an empty filesystem).
2. `EwmaEstimator::update` calls `sample.clamp(0.0, 1.0)`. **`NaN.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` returns NaN** — this is documented Rust behaviour but caller-hostile. The clamp does not sanitise the NaN.
3. `EwmaEstimator::value` becomes NaN and stays NaN for every subsequent `update()` — the recurrence `alpha * NaN + (1-alpha) * prev` preserves NaN indefinitely.
4. `PidController::update` receives `pid_error = NaN - target`. Its internal integral + derivative go NaN and the output is NaN. Permanent until `reset()`.
5. `classify_tier(NaN, thresholds)` compares `NaN >= thresholds.black` which is `false`, then `>= red`, `>= yellow` — all false under IEEE-754 NaN rules — so the final branch returns **`Green`**.
6. Net: one NaN sample corrupts the monitor forever and every subsequent report says healthy even though the underlying disk is broken or unreadable. Operators trusting the Green tier take no action.

## Severity rationale

- Silent wrong answer, not a crash or a visible error.
- Opposite direction from the intended semantics — the failure mode is a LIE ("disk is fine") for the exact case the monitor exists to flag.
- One bad sample is sticky: the monitor cannot self-recover.

## Fix sketch

1. `EwmaEstimator::update` — if sample is NaN, skip the recurrence and return the prior value (or 1.0 if uninitialised). Do not poison internal state.
2. `PidController::update` — if error is NaN, skip state update and return 0.0 (or last output). Same no-poison rule.
3. `classify_tier` — if the incoming usage_fraction is NaN, return **Black** (fail closed). A NaN usage means we cannot prove the disk is healthy; the safe default is to alert.
4. Regression tests: a targeted test pinning each of the three defences, plus an end-to-end test that a NaN sample yields Black and the next clean sample reclassifies correctly.

## Scope

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/disk_pressure.rs` only. No cross-crate API changes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:39:31.506608Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:14.150284Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:50:14.149967Z","close_reason":"Landed in c5a02edb (fix(disk_pressure): fail closed on NaN in Ewma, PID, and classifier) + regression tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-icreu","title":"[MEDIUM] ActivityProfile::new allows NaN alpha/default_activity past clamp; poisons all hourly_ewma slots","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/survival.rs:437-438` — `ActivityProfile::new`.

ft-26k3h just hardened `update_hour` against NaN input. The construction-time path is the **complement**: `alpha.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` and `default_activity.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` both still let NaN through because `f64::clamp(NaN, _, _)` returns NaN.

Two independent failure modes:

1. **NaN `default_activity`**: all 24 `hourly_ewma` slots are initialised to NaN. `predict_hour` returns NaN for every slot until the first clean `update_hour` overwrites the bucket via the `sample_count == 0` branch. Until then, every hazard/survival score computed from `predict` silently produces NaN (matches the ft-26k3h failure shape but from a different path).

2. **NaN `alpha`**: `self.alpha = NaN`. `update_hour` at line 460 computes `alpha.mul_add(sample, (1.0 - alpha) * prev) = NaN` on every non-first sample. `hourly_ewma[idx]` stays NaN from the second sample onward. ft-26k3h's entry guard doesn't catch this because the caller passes a clean `normalized_activity` — the NaN is stored in `self.alpha`, not in the input.

Both are the same `f64::clamp(NaN)` gotcha shipped across ft-761tz / ft-b4l62 / ft-jz40p / ft-0p5q5 / ft-x50ce / ft-26k3h. This closes the pair's construction-time counterpart.

## Fix

Snap NaN to sensible defaults at the boundary:
- `alpha` → 0.1 (a conservative EWMA coefficient; config-adjacent code uses 0.3 as the common default but 0.1 is safe for an unspecified sentinel).
- `default_activity` → 0.5 (neutral midpoint — if operator didn't supply a meaningful default, 0.5 is the least-surprising fallback).

Both `.is_nan()` checks happen **before** the clamp so the clamp still trims out-of-range finite inputs normally.

## Tests

* `ft_icreu_new_sanitises_nan_alpha` — alpha=NaN → stored α=0.1, EWMA updates stay finite.
* `ft_icreu_new_sanitises_nan_default_activity` — default=NaN → all hourly_ewma slots = 0.5, predict_hour returns 0.5.

## Scope

Single file (`survival.rs`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:55:55.543754Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:58:20.995785Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:58:20.995504Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-ih4tm","title":"[MEDIUM] defer_fts_triggers=false is not reversible: triggers stay dropped after enabling once (ft-wk5fo follow-up)","description":"# Defect

## Concrete failure path

File: `crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs`.

d74401c6 landed `StorageConfig.defer_fts_triggers` (ft-wk5fo). The intent is a reversible opt-in: flip to `true` to trade FTS freshness for write-path latency, flip back to `false` to restore synchronous indexing. The inline comment at line 6006 says as much:

> The triggers can be re-created by re-opening with `defer_fts_triggers: false`

That claim is wrong. Concrete interleaving:

1. Operator opens DB with `defer_fts_triggers: true` on an up-to-date schema (`user_version == SCHEMA_VERSION`).
2. `initialize_schema` returns early at line 4315 (no SQL executed, up-to-date path).
3. The explicit `DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS output_segments_ai/ad/au` at line 6010 runs — triggers gone.
4. Operator flips config to `defer_fts_triggers: false` and restarts.
5. `initialize_schema` again short-circuits at line 4315.
6. Line 6010 is skipped (flag is false). **No `CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS` runs.**
7. Database silently stays in deferred mode; the operator's `false` setting is a no-op.

The inline comment at line 6007 hints the author knew: 'this commit ships the opt-in direction only; reverting would require re-running SCHEMA_SQL or the operator dropping the DB.' That matches reality but contradicts the line-6006 'can be re-created by re-opening' claim — and leaves the config behaving non-intuitively for operators.

## Severity rationale

- Not a crash or data loss.
- Silent wrong answer: operator flips back to `false`, expects synchronous FTS, gets deferred.
- One-way-door config is a known anti-pattern. Easy fix.

## Fix sketch

Make the open path truly bidirectional. On every open, after `initialize_schema` returns:

* If `defer_fts_triggers == true`: `DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS ...` (idempotent).
* If `defer_fts_triggers == false`: `CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS ...` using the same SQL the SCHEMA_SQL block defines.

Factor the trigger SQL into one private const referenced by both branches so SCHEMA_SQL and the re-create branch cannot drift.

## Tests

* ft_ih4tm_defer_fts_triggers_roundtrip — open with true, verify triggers dropped, close, reopen with false, verify triggers re-created.
* ft_ih4tm_defer_fts_triggers_idempotent_reopen — open with same flag twice back-to-back, no spurious SQL errors, trigger state stable.

## Scope

Single file (`storage.rs`). No API break (`defer_fts_triggers` already public, default `false`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:27:12.433144Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:31:50.095316Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:31:50.095017Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-ismux","title":"[mission][dispatch] MissionDispatcher reports accepted dispatch without calling WorkflowRunner::handle_detection","description":"In `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_dispatch.rs:200-226`, `dispatch_single()` builds a synthetic detection, then only calls `runner.find_matching_workflow(&detection)` and fabricates `accepted: true` plus a synthetic `execution_id` when a workflow exists. It never invokes `WorkflowRunner::handle_detection()`, so no lock acquisition, no concurrency-limit enforcement, no storage row, and no real workflow start happen.

Impact: callers receive a success-shaped `DispatchResult` that claims work was accepted even though the runner was never asked to start anything. This also makes the existing close reason on `ft-y9lnb.3` overstated relative to the current code.

Fix direction: bridge the sync mission loop to the async runner start path with a bounded runtime, map the actual `WorkflowStartResult` into `DispatchResult`, and only emit started metadata from a real start result.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:05:13.673841Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:13:49.575203Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:13:49.574685Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of ft-4r2ib. crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_dispatch.rs is already dirty in this checkout with the exact handle_detection/WorkflowStartResult fix and regression coverage, so I did not collide with pane-4-owned WIP.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-jqvg5","title":"Resolve frankenterm-core clippy -D warnings gate","description":"# Workspace-level clippy gate

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 356 verification sweep. The
`frankenterm-core` crate reports **17 clippy errors** when run with
`-D warnings`:

    cargo clippy --no-deps -p frankenterm-core \\
        --features distributed,asupersync-runtime,web \\
        --lib --tests -- -D warnings

All 17 errors are in files **unrelated to ft-xbnl0.2.4**:

- `src/ipc.rs`
- `src/robot_sdk_contracts.rs`
- `src/runtime.rs`
- `src/snapshot_engine.rs`
- `src/ui_query.rs`
- `src/workflows/engine.rs`
- `src/workflows/runner.rs`

These are from recent commits by other agents.

## Why not fix in ft-xbnl0.2.4
AGENTS.md explicitly instructs "NEVER ... disturb in ANY way the work of
other agents." The violations may be:
- awaiting `#[allow(...)]` annotations from their owners
- pending intentional restructures
- already planned for cleanup

Touching them without coordination could override the owners' planned
solutions.

## Fix categories observed (examples)
- `clippy::duration-suboptimal-units` — `Duration::from_millis(2_000)` should be `from_secs(2)`
- `clippy::redundant-closure` — `|| crate::foo()` should just be `crate::foo`
- `clippy::semicolon-if-nothing-returned` — trailing unit-valued expression without semicolon
- Several others (see `cargo clippy` output at time of fix)

## Suggested approach
For each file, coordinate with the owning agent (via Agent Mail or by checking
recent git history for active work) before applying fixes. Prefer:
1. Fixing real issues over `#[allow(...)]` annotations.
2. Single-file commits so each fix is easy to review.
3. Running `ubs` on each file after the clippy fix to avoid introducing
   new bugs in the cleanup.

## Not a blocker for ft-xbnl0.2.4
The ft-xbnl0.2.4-scoped files (`src/distributed.rs`, `src/metrics.rs`,
`src/runtime_compat.rs`, `tests/web.rs`, `tests/ft_xbnl0_2_4_*.rs`) produce
zero clippy warnings.

See: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §6.1.1`.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"FoggyRobin","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:04:55.194692Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-19T15:17:33.170677Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T15:17:33.170424Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["clippy","code-quality","workspace-gate"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-jqvg5","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T18:05:03.540304Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"ft-jz40p","title":"[MEDIUM] TimeSeries::percentile(NaN) returns smallest value instead of None","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/time_series.rs:239` — `TimeSeries::percentile_range` (and the `percentile` convenience that delegates to it).

Same class as ft-b4l62 (telemetry Histogram::quantile, fixed 2c7b891c). The function relies on `p.clamp(0.0, 1.0)` to validate its input, but `f64::clamp(NaN, _, _)` returns NaN (documented Rust behaviour), so a NaN `p` cascades through:

```rust
let p = p.clamp(0.0, 1.0);                          // NaN stays NaN
let idx = ((values.len() - 1) as f64 * p)           // NaN
    .round() as usize;                              // NaN as usize = 0
Some(values[idx])                                   // smallest value
```

A NaN quantile arg silently returns the smallest sample — a plausible-looking number that has no relationship to the requested percentile. Pre-fix the Histogram analog had the same bug; the fix pattern is identical.

## Fix

```rust
if values.is_empty() || p.is_nan() {
    return None;
}
```

Matches the ft-b4l62 disposition: NaN `p` is explicitly `None` rather than a silently-wrong small value.

## Tests

* `ft_jz40p_percentile_nan_returns_none` — pins None return for NaN p.
* `ft_jz40p_percentile_infinity_inputs_still_work` — regression: ±inf still clamp to 0.0/1.0 and return min/max samples (pre-existing contract, not broken by the new NaN guard).

## Scope

Single file (`time_series.rs`).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane1","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:11:47.750602Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:22:17.756502Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:22:17.756187Z","close_reason":"Fix and both regression tests already landed (time_series.rs:246-254 + :720-745). percentile_range now guards with 'if values.is_empty() || p.is_nan() { return None; }' at line 252, so percentile(NaN) returns None instead of silently returning the smallest sample via NaN-clamp-to-0 → (NaN as usize)==0 cascade. Regression tests: ft_jz40p_percentile_nan_returns_none (NaN→None on both percentile + percentile_range, plus sanity check that valid p=0.5 still works) and ft_jz40p_percentile_infinity_inputs_still_work (±inf continue clamping to 0.0/1.0 → min/max sample, pre-existing contract preserved). Verified: 38 time_series:: tests passed, 0 failed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-l6v1r","title":"[mor:distributed-systems] missed PaneDestroyed leaks tier/state maps across the swarm","description":"# Observation

`PaneTierClassifier` holds `RwLock<HashMap<u64, PaneState>>`
(crates/frankenterm-core/src/pane_tiers.rs:238). Entries are added in
`register_pane` and removed in `unregister_pane`. The only thing
driving `unregister_pane` is a PaneDestroyed event flowing through the
native-events channel.

That channel has three failure modes documented in
`dispatch_event_with_timeout_with_cx`:
- `EventDispatchOutcome::Sent` — happy path
- `EventDispatchOutcome::Backpressure` — timeout expired, event dropped
- `EventDispatchOutcome::Closed` — channel gone

Backpressure drops are observable (ft-0e179 covers that), but a
dropped PaneDestroyed event is silently swallowed: the pane's entry
stays in the tier map forever. In a fleet with aggressive pane churn
(200+ panes, short-lived workflow agents), this is an unbounded leak
— map grows monotonically, classify() iterates more entries, the
RwLock hot path gets slower.

# Question

Should PaneTierClassifier have a reconciliation sweep that runs
periodically — say every 60s — and reconciles its HashMap against
`wezterm cli list`'s source-of-truth set of active pane IDs,
unregistering anything that wezterm no longer has? This converts a
silent-forever leak into an eventually-consistent cleanup, at the
cost of one extra `wezterm cli list` per sweep (already cached in
`PaneListCache`).

Same leak shape likely exists in other per-pane maps
(backpressure.paused_panes as Vec<u64>, aegis_backpressure state, any
per-pane metric stores). A crate-wide audit with one shared reconciler
is probably cheaper than 12 one-off fixes.

# CAP stance

Current design is "consistency by event delivery." On paper, the
event stream is authoritative. In practice, bounded queues mean
delivery is not guaranteed. A ground-truth reconciler trades the
"instantaneous" property for "eventually matches reality" — better

 {"id":"ft-l9mxa","title":"DistributedHttpClient response-read path does not observe mid-flight cx-cancel","description":"# DistributedHttpClient mid-flight cx-cancel gap

## Background
Discovered during ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 380 verification work. When a caller fires
`cx.cancel_with(...)` WHILE a `DistributedHttpClient::get` is in flight (blocked
reading response bytes from a stalled server), the cancel does NOT propagate to
the HTTP client's read loop.

## Reproduction
The ft-xbnl0.2.4 test
`distributed::tests::distributed_http_client_mid_flight_cancel_does_not_hang`
exercises this. Observed behavior:

    elapsed = 10.02s
    result = Err("deadline has elapsed at Time(10000000041ns)")

The elapsed = ~10s matches the test's DEFENSIVE outer timeout ceiling
(`crate::runtime_compat::timeout(10s, client.get(...))`), not the 50 ms
cx-cancel fire. The cancel went through (cx.is_cancel_requested() would return
true) but the HTTP client's await points didn't observe it.

## Relation to other findings
- **Tick 328** documented that `timeout_with_cx` observes cx *budget deadline*
  but not direct cx *cancel*. This is the same shape of gap at a different
  layer — the HTTP client wraps asupersync::http::h1 whose await points
  don't poll cx.
- **ft-kfkyi** (closed tick 351) was a distinct concern (3xx redirect
  exfil). This finding is lower-severity: not a security issue, just an
  operational-ergonomics gap.

## Impact
- Hung distributed RPC calls will eventually time out via internal limits
  (if any configured) but won't observe operator-initiated cancel.
- Agent workflows that fire cx.cancel to abort a slow peer call will wait
  the full internal-timeout window before the call returns.

## Fix approach (candidate paths)
1. Extend `DistributedHttpClient::get/post` to race the inner future against
   a cx-cancel future (tokio::select! or similar race).
2. Patch asupersync::http::h1 to thread cx through its await points and
   honor cancel at read/write boundaries. Bigger scope; upstream change.
3. Wrap the DistributedHttpClient inner calls in `timeout_with_cx` —
   but per tick 328, that only observes budget, not cancel. So this
   doesn't help unless the caller sets a short budget.

Option (1) is the narrowest change; aligns with the cx-first migration
pattern and doesn't require upstream asupersync work.

## Verification
The existing tick-380 snapshot test is the flip trigger. When the fix lands:
1. Observed elapsed drops from ~10s to << 10s (cx-cancel observed mid-read).
2. `result` shape changes from the outer-timeout Err to the HTTP client's
   own cancel-shaped Err.
3. Update the snapshot assertions to require the faster behavior.

## References
- Finding: ft-xbnl0.2.4 tick 380 (commit 4d925607)
- Related: `docs/ft-xbnl0-2-4-completion-evidence.md §5` out-of-scope carve-out
  for primitive-doc-only coverage (timeout_with_cx / sleep_with_cx — ticks
  328 / 331).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-18T20:12:43.612639Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-18T20:50:48.342908Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T20:50:48.342659Z","close_reason":"Fixed by tick 387 (commit 99640e41): DistributedHttpClient::{get,post} now race inner call against cx-cancel watcher (50ms poll). Mid-flight cancel surfaces as Ok(Err(Cancelled)) in ~70ms. Tick-380 snapshot test tightened to assert the new fast-cancel behavior (elapsed < 1s, exact Cancelled variant).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cx-cancel","distributed","http"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-l9mxa","depends_on_id":"ft-xbnl0.2.4","type":"related","created_at":"2026-04-18T20:12:58.856854Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

- :1216 wa.get_text (Mux, read)
- :1626 wa.search (Mux, read)
- :2184 wa.send (Mux, write — deny path; PolicyGatedInjector already audits the success path to audit_actions, different table)
- :2435 wa.workflow_run (Workflow, write)
- :3385 wa.reserve (Swarm)
- :3536 wa.release (Swarm)
- :3795 wa.accounts_refresh (Swarm)

## Scope

Each of the 7 handler call() methods already lives inside a `runtime.block_on(async { ... })` with a StorageHandle in scope, so the async `storage.record_policy_denial_audit(record).await` path works directly — no blocking bridge needed like the one added in storage.rs:record_policy_denial_audit_blocking for the sync gate.

Pattern per site:

    let decision = engine.authorize(&input);
    if decision.is_denied() {
        let reason = policy_reason(&decision).unwrap_or("...").to_string();
        let record = PolicyDeniedAuditRecord {
            id: 0,
            ts_ms: i64::try_from(now_ms()).unwrap_or(0),
            agent_id: None,
            tool_name: "wa.get_text".to_string(),
            intent_hash: Some(intent_hash_hex(&summary)),
            reason: reason.clone(),
            reason_code: PolicyDeniedAuditRecord::REASON_CODE_DENIED.to_string(),
            rule_id: decision.rule_id().map(String::from),
            decision: PolicyDeniedAuditRecord::DECISION_DENIED.to_string(),
        };
        let _ = storage.record_policy_denial_audit(record).await;
        tracing::warn!(target: "ft::security::policy", ...);
        return Err(...);
    }

## Blocker

Pane 2's ft-eu0no is still uncommitted (+70 lines on mcp_tools.rs touching exactly the 4 non-gate tools: WaGetText/WaSearch/WaSend/WaWorkflowRun). The other 3 (wa.reserve/release/accounts_refresh) may be safe to wire now but splitting the 7 across two beads would be bead-churn.

- docs/security/policy-denial-audit-wiring-matrix.md status column flipped to ✓ on all 8 rows.
